# Play Store Submission & Policy Checklist (GastRotator)

This document summarizes the steps and disclosures required to comply with Google Play Store policies as of April 2026.

## 1. Generative AI Disclosure (MANDATORY)
In the Play Console, you must complete the **Generative AI** declaration. 
*   **App Content:** Declare that your app "provides content generated by AI" (the recipe extraction).
*   **Safety Features:** GastRotator uses the Gemini API, which has built-in safety filters. 
*   **Reporting Mechanism:** Point to the "Report an Issue" link in the **About** screen.

## 2. Data Safety Section
When filling out the Data Safety form in the Play Console, use these values:
*   **Data Collection:** No (We do not collect user data).
*   **Data Shared:** No (We do not share data with third parties).
*   **Security Practices:** Declare that data is "encrypted in transit" (HTTPS) and that users can request data deletion (by deleting recipes or the app).

## 3. Brand Compliance (Store Listing)
*   **Title:** Keep as **GastRotator**. Do NOT use "YouTube" in the title.
*   **Short Description:** "AI-powered recipe manager to organize your favorite cooking videos." (Avoid brand names here).
*   **Full Description:** Focus on the "Culinary Curator" experience. Use "Import from supported video platforms" instead of focusing exclusively on YouTube.

## 4. AI Content Reporting
*   **Action Required:** Ensure the GitHub repository link in the **About** screen is updated to the new repository URL before final submission. This fulfills the policy requiring a "user reporting mechanism" for AI content.

## 5. Metadata and Icons
*   **App Icon:** We have updated the in-app icon style. Ensure the `launcher_icon` used for the APK matches the new "About Screen" aesthetic (Themed square, rounded corners).
*   **Screenshots:** Ensure screenshots do not show copyrighted material (use generic recipe placeholders or your own cooking videos).
